name: Priority First
description_short: Priority First Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards with personalized service across 7 locations.
description_long:
Priority First Federal Credit Union has served its membership since 1969, establishing itself as a community-focused financial institution with a membership base of approximately 16,000 members and $150 million in assets. The credit union operates 7 physical branch locations and emphasizes personalized, local service as its primary differentiator in the competitive financial services market.

Priority First offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking and lending products. Members can access share certificates (savings products), checking accounts (share draft accounts), personal auto loans (as low as 4.99% APR), home equity lines of credit (starting at 5.99%), in-house mortgage origination and servicing, VISA Platinum credit cards (as low as 8.90% APR), and various other consumer loans. The credit union provides online and mobile banking platforms available 24/7, bill pay services, eStatements, and the ability to download transaction data in CSV or OFX formats.

Key distinguishing features include a stated commitment to eliminating call-center operations—member calls are handled by staff at local branches rather than centralized phone centers. The credit union emphasizes no holds or transfers when contacting the organization. Additionally, Priority First recently expanded its mortgage services in-house, allowing members to access home financing directly through the credit union with "faster decisions and personal support from start to finish."

The membership consists primarily of individuals within the credit union's service area who qualify for membership. While the credit union provides standard banking services, it operates as a regional institution with limited branch presence (7 locations), which may restrict accessibility for those outside its geographic footprint. The credit union does not advertise specialized lending programs for members with challenged credit, nor does it promote itself as a credit-building institution.
services: Share certificates (savings products with variable rates)
Home equity lines of credit (from 5.99% APR)
Online banking with account history review and transaction reconciliation
Direct deposit and money transfers between accounts
pros: No call-center model ensures direct contact with knowledgeable local staff without extended holds or transfers
In-house mortgage origination provides faster decisions and personalized support for home purchase and refinance
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.99% APR for new vehicles
24/7 online and mobile banking with transaction download capability in multiple formats (CSV, OFX)
Member-owned structure with 55+ years of community presence since 1969
Free mobile banking app with secure account access and transaction management
Bill pay service available through online banking for members with share draft (checking) accounts
cons: Limited branch network (7 locations) restricts in-person service accessibility for members outside service area
No indication of specialized credit-building products or programs for members with fair/poor credit histories
Higher credit card APR floor (8.90%) compared to some competitors' promotional offers
Website does not clearly communicate membership eligibility criteria or geographic service boundaries
No mention of payday alternative loans (PALs) or emergency lending products for urgent cash needs
best_for: Local community members seeking personalized service and relationship banking without automated call centers
First-time homebuyers needing streamlined in-house mortgage origination and support
Existing members looking to consolidate banking relationships (auto loans, mortgages, credit cards) with one institution
Individuals seeking competitive rates on auto loans and home equity products in the credit union's service area
diagnosis: Priority First is best suited for consumers in its 7-location service area who prioritize personalized service and relationship banking over digital-first convenience, and who need standard consumer lending products like auto loans and mortgages. The main caveat is geographic limitation—the credit union's value proposition depends entirely on local branch access, making it unsuitable for members outside its physical footprint or those who prefer fully digital banking experiences.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
